#1 QLYS Mid Cap Qualys, Inc. · Technology HIGH ACT NOW Quality Compounder Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Qualys provides cloud-based vulnerability management and security compliance software, delivered as a SaaS platform to enterprises globally. Customers pay recurring subscriptions for continuous monitoring of their attack surface — servers, endpoints, cloud infrastructure. Revenue is highly sticky because Qualys is embedded in compliance workflows that carry regulatory and audit consequences if replaced.

Industry: Cloud Security / Vulnerability Management  ·  vs: Tenable Holdings, Rapid7, Microsoft Defender (Vulnerability Management module)

"The market conflates slow revenue growth with business deterioration — 46% FCF margins and 28% ROIC at 15x PE is not a melting ice cube, it is a toll road."

Six independent specialist lenses — the highest consensus in this entire dataset — all identify the same mispricing: the market has re-categorised Qualys from 'quality SaaS' to 'legacy VM vendor' based solely on 10% revenue growth deceleration, ignoring that FCF margins of 46% and ROIC of 28% are elite metrics that the vast majority of software companies never achieve. At PE 15.4 with net cash (NDE -0.6x), the business trades at roughly the same multiple as a utility while generating 5x the returns on capital. Insiders are buying aggressively at 54% of the 52-week high, signalling the AI-enhanced CTEM/EASM platform refresh is not priced in.

Price $83.17 PEG 1.6 P/E 15.4 ROIC 28.1% Catalyst CTEM/EASM product upsell cycle visible in Q2 2026 earnings (expected July 2026) — management has guided for re-acceleration in contract values as the AI-enhanced platform commands premium pricing tiers; insider buying pattern over the past 3 months provides near-term confirmation signal. Watch Microsoft bundles vulnerability management more aggressively into E5 licensing, commoditising the entry-level market and forcing Qualys to defend on price rather than compliance depth.

#2 PTCT Mid Cap PTC Therapeutics, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH ACT NOW Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

PTC Therapeutics is a commercial-stage rare disease biopharmaceutical company with approved therapies in Duchenne muscular dystrophy and spinal muscular atrophy. Revenue is generated through direct drug sales and royalties, with the risdiplam royalty from Roche providing high-margin recurring income. The company has 91% gross margins reflecting the pricing power inherent in treating ultra-rare diseases with few alternatives.

Industry: Rare Disease / Specialty Pharma  ·  vs: Sarepta Therapeutics, Biogen (SMA franchise), Novartis (Zolgensma)

"A 37% ROIC rare-disease franchise generating 40% FCF margins is priced at PE 8.7 because the Street still models it as a speculative single-asset biotech."

Five specialist lenses converge on the same thesis: PTCT has already completed its transformation from a speculative drug developer to a cash-generating rare disease compounder, yet the market continues applying a pre-profitability biotech discount. Revenue grew 115% YoY (from -14% the prior year) as newly launched therapies hit formulary coverage, and the risdiplam royalty stream from Roche alone plausibly justifies near the current market cap — meaning the SMA pipeline and DMD franchise are effectively free. At 37% ROIC with 40% FCF margins and net cash, this is a financials profile comparable to best-in-class software businesses.

Price $72.43 PEG — P/E 8.7 ROIC 37.4% Catalyst Continued formulary expansion for newly approved therapies through H1 2026, plus SMA pipeline readout and potential label expansion data expected Q3 2026 — any positive regulatory interaction resets the peer group multiple from 'biotech' to 'specialty pharma' (typically 15-20x earnings vs current 8.7x). Watch Risdiplam royalty rate renegotiation with Roche at contract renewal — if Roche exercises pricing leverage to reduce the royalty rate, the highest-margin revenue stream contracts materially.

#3 DUOL Mid Cap Duolingo, Inc. · Technology HIGH ACT NOW Fast Grower Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Duolingo operates the world's leading mobile language-learning platform, monetised primarily through subscription (Duolingo Max/Super) and advertising. Over 500 million registered users generate data that powers AI-personalised lesson sequencing. The company is expanding into mathematics and music education, leveraging its gamified engagement model and brand.

Industry: Consumer EdTech / AI-Personalised Learning  ·  vs: Babbel, Rosetta Stone (Cambium Learning), Busuu (McGraw-Hill)

"The market is punishing Duolingo at 18% of its 52-week high for a growth scare that has not materialised — a 39% revenue grower with 36% FCF margins at PEG 0.7 is mispriced by a factor of 2-3x."

Five specialist lenses agree: the market applied a peak-EdTech-bubble derating to Duolingo that is completely disconnected from the underlying business, which has since inflected to 36% FCF margins and 39% revenue growth. At PEG 0.7 with 75% DCF margin of safety and 22% net cash-to-price, this is not a speculative growth stock — it is a profitable, cash-generating platform with the dominant global brand in mobile language learning and zero credible competition at scale. The ROIC at 9% is a legitimate concern (below the 15% preferred threshold) but reflects the early-stage conversion of reinvestment into margin; operating margins are expanding from 13%, and FCF conversion is what matters here at a 36% margin — which comfortably clears the 4.32% rate hurdle.

Price $100.51 PEG 0.7 P/E 11.1 ROIC 9.4% Catalyst Duolingo Max AI tier pricing power validation in Q2 2026 earnings report (expected August 2026) — if ARPU acceleration from Max subscribers is visible in reported metrics, the market re-rates from 'slowing EdTech' to 'AI-monetised consumer platform'; stock at 18% of 52-week high means any positive earnings surprise has disproportionate upside. Watch Dilution risk — shares outstanding growing at 5%/year (borderline for 10-Bagger quality gate); if stock-based compensation does not decline as a percentage of revenue as margins scale, the FCF per share improvement thesis is diluted away.

#4 HRB Mid Cap H&R Block, Inc. · Financial Services HIGH WITHIN WEEKS IV Discount Slow Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

H&R Block is the largest consumer tax preparation company in the United States, operating approximately 9,000 retail locations and a digital filing platform. Revenue is generated through paid tax preparation fees and financial products (refund advances, Emerald Card). The company serves ~20 million US tax filers annually and has expanded into small business services and bookkeeping.

Industry: Consumer Financial Services / Tax Preparation  ·  vs: Intuit TurboTax, Jackson Hewitt, IRS Direct File (government program)

"The market has been pricing IRS Direct File existential risk for two years — but H&R Block's 30% ROIC and 6% annual buyback yield prove the business is not dying, it is compounding quietly."

Four specialist lenses identify the same permanent-discount mispricing: Wall Street has been penalising HRB for a free-filing disruption threat that has consistently failed to materialise at scale. IRS Direct File serves simple returns; H&R Block's moat is in complexity — self-employed filers, small businesses, multi-state returns, and Spanish-language services — none of which Direct File addresses. At PE 6.4 with 30% ROIC, 76% DCF margin of safety, and shares retiring at 6%/year, the equity is mathematically self-liquidating in under 10 years while paying a 5.3% dividend yield. Insiders buying near decade lows is the clearest signal that management sees the same math.

Price $32.16 PEG 0.5 P/E 6.4 ROIC 30.1% Catalyst FY2026 tax season results (typically reported May/June 2026) — if assisted return volumes grow 2%+ and digital attach rates for financial products improve, the IRS Direct File threat narrative collapses and the stock re-rates from PE 6.4 toward 10-12x, a 50-90% return from current levels. Watch Congress mandating free federal filing for all income levels would be the genuine existential catalyst — monitor tax legislation in the FY2027 budget reconciliation process; this is unlikely but is the single binary risk worth tracking.
Agents: 🔢 Magic Formula 👁️ Insider Track 🛒 Lynch BWYK ⚡ Special Sit
#5 DLO Mid Cap DLocal Limited · Technology HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

DLocal operates a single-API payment processing platform enabling global merchants to accept and disburse payments across 40+ emerging markets in Latin America, Africa, and Asia. Revenue is generated as a percentage of transaction volume (take rate). Customers include Amazon, Microsoft, Uber, and Spotify — the platform handles local payment methods, compliance, and FX conversion for each market.

Industry: Emerging Market Payments Infrastructure  ·  vs: Adyen (EM expansion), Stripe (limited EM coverage), EBANX (LATAM specialist)

"The market permanently de-rated DLocal on a one-quarter take-rate compression scare in 2023 and has never re-rated despite 47% revenue growth resumption and 29% ROIC — it is priced as a distressed fintech when it is actually the dominant EM payments OS."

Four specialist lenses converge: DLocal was unfairly punished for a 2023 take-rate compression quarter that reflected deliberate volume scaling with tier-1 merchants, not structural margin erosion. The business has since demonstrated 47% revenue growth re-acceleration (from 15% the prior year), 37% FCF margins, 29% ROIC, and 18% net cash as a percentage of market cap. At PEG 0.9 with 84% DCF margin of safety, this is the rarest combination in growth investing: a genuinely high-quality business at a value price. The competitive moat — a single API covering local payment methods, regulatory compliance, and FX in 40+ markets — would take a competitor 5-7 years and $500M+ to replicate.

Price $13.68 PEG 0.9 P/E 20.3 ROIC 28.5% Catalyst Africa segment revenue contribution first disclosed in Q2 2026 earnings (expected July/August 2026) — if African market volumes are showing 50%+ growth, the TAM expansion narrative resets the peer group comparison from 'LATAM fintech' to 'global EM payments infrastructure', warranting a significant multiple re-rating. Watch USD strengthening materially against BRL/MXN/ARS — DLocal's reported USD revenue is sensitive to FX translation, and a strong-dollar shock would compress reported growth rates regardless of underlying volume growth, potentially triggering another sentiment-driven sell-off.

#6 NICE Mid Cap NICE Ltd. · Technology HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Turnaround Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

NICE Systems provides AI-powered cloud contact centre software (CXone platform) and financial crime compliance solutions to large enterprises globally. Revenue is generated through SaaS subscriptions with multi-year contracts. The customer base includes over 85% of Fortune 100 companies across banking, insurance, and retail sectors who use NICE for customer interaction management and regulatory compliance recording.

Industry: Enterprise AI / Contact Centre SaaS  ·  vs: Genesys, Five9, Salesforce Service Cloud, AWS Connect

"The market is valuing NICE as a legacy on-premise vendor being disrupted by cloud — it is actually the incumbent platform enterprises are migrating TO, with a 83% DCF margin of safety and PEG 0.5."

Four specialist lenses identify NICE as a textbook Marks Second-Level opportunity: consensus reads 'legacy contact centre vendor = disrupted' but the underlying data tells the opposite story. NICE CXone IS the cloud platform driving enterprise migration away from Avaya and Genesys on-premise installations. At PE 10.4 (on growing earnings), PEG 0.5, 66% gross margins, 100% revenue consistency, and a net cash balance sheet, NICE meets every quality gate while trading at 42% of its 52-week high. The re-rating catalyst is mechanical: as cloud revenue mix crosses 70%+ of total, the market is forced to re-categorise NICE as a SaaS business, not a legacy software company.

Price $105.25 PEG 0.5 P/E 10.4 ROIC 13.3% Catalyst H2 2026 earnings re-rating as cloud revenue mix becomes dominant in reported financials — management has guided for this inflection; additionally, any acceleration in Avaya customer migrations (Avaya is in ongoing financial distress) provides a named, trackable catalyst for NICE seat additions. Watch Salesforce Service Cloud AI acceleration — if Salesforce's Einstein AI agent capabilities commoditise the AI contact centre layer, NICE's premium pricing in that segment faces structural pressure; watch Salesforce's Q2 2026 Service Cloud seat growth as the leading indicator.

#7 RNR Large Cap RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd. · Financial Services HIGH WITHIN WEEKS IV Discount Stalwart Asset Play ▼
🏢 About the Business

RenaissanceRe is a global reinsurance company specialising in property catastrophe and casualty reinsurance. Revenue is generated through premium underwriting, with additional fee income from managing $7B+ in third-party capital via joint ventures and sidecars. The company is known for its proprietary catastrophe modelling capability, which allows more precise risk pricing than competitors.

Industry: Specialty Reinsurance / Catastrophe Risk  ·  vs: Everest Re, Munich Re, Swiss Re

"Trading at PE 5.2 with ROE 24% and insiders buying at 52-week highs, RenaissanceRe is the highest-quality catastrophe reinsurer in the world at a trough multiple in what remains a hard market."

Three specialist lenses — including deep value and asymmetric bet frameworks — converge on the same structural mispricing: the market is applying a trough-cycle multiple to RNR because of catastrophe loss headlines, but the underlying business fundamentals are peak-cycle. Hard market conditions (tight reinsurance capacity, rising rates) persist structurally due to climate volatility and capacity shortage — RNR's proprietary catastrophe models let it price risk more accurately than Munich Re or Swiss Re, generating structurally superior combined ratios. The 27% FCF yield and 12% annual buyback dramatically exceed the 4.32% risk-free rate hurdle. Insiders buying at 52-week highs (100% of 52-week high price) signals internal conviction that loss reserve adequacy is not a concern.

Price $315.20 PEG 0.6 P/E 5.2 ROIC 8.1% Catalyst Q1 2026 earnings season (May 2026) — if cat loss experience is within model expectations after a relatively benign Q1 hurricane season start, the market removes the 'pending cat loss' discount and re-rates toward 8-10x earnings (50-100% upside from PE 5.2); insider buying pattern at 52-week highs is a leading confirmation signal. Watch A major catastrophe event (Atlantic hurricane season June-November 2026, or California earthquake) causing losses above the 1-in-20-year level would reset the earnings trajectory and justify a sustained lower multiple — this is the primary binary risk.

#8 BAH Mid Cap Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation · Industrials HIGH WITHIN MONTHS Turnaround Stalwart Fast Grower Cyclical Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Booz Allen Hamilton is a management and IT consulting firm serving primarily the US federal government, with specialisation in defence, intelligence, and civilian agency digital transformation. Revenue is primarily from cost-reimbursable and fixed-price US government contracts. The company provides cybersecurity, data analytics, AI, and engineering services to classified and unclassified government programs.

Industry: Government IT Services / Defence Consulting  ·  vs: Leidos, SAIC, Accenture Federal Services

"DOGE fear has created an 85% margin of safety on a business where 70%+ of revenues are classified defence programs that political budget cuts literally cannot legally touch."

Three specialist lenses identify the same DOGE-driven mispricing: the market has extrapolated visible civilian agency budget cuts to BAH's entire revenue base, but the majority of BAH's work is classified intelligence and defence work funded through multi-year appropriations that operate entirely outside the civilian DOGE scope. At 62% of its 52-week high, PE 12.1, ROIC 18%, 100% revenue consistency, and 85% DCF margin of safety, this is a case study in second-level thinking. The 2.7% dividend and continued capital returns signal management confidence that the revenue base is not impaired.

Price $81.77 PEG 1.2 P/E 12.1 ROIC 18.1% Catalyst Q1 FY2027 earnings (expected July/August 2026) plus Congressional FY2027 defence appropriations markup — if BAH reports contract awards above $2B in the quarter (tracking the run rate) and defence appropriations are passed with real increases, the DOGE discount evaporates mechanically. Watch Continuing resolution (CR) funding freeze extending beyond Q3 FY2026 — CRs prevent new contract starts and force agencies into minimal operations, which does affect BAH's new business pipeline even for defence programs; a prolonged CR scenario delays revenue recognition even if the underlying spending authority exists.
Agents: 👁️ Insider Track 🔄 Marks 2nd-Level 🎲 Pabrai Asym.
#9 ODD Small Cap Oddity Tech Ltd. · Consumer Cyclical HIGH WITHIN MONTHS 10-Bagger Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

Oddity Tech is an Israeli-founded, AI-native direct-to-consumer beauty platform operating IL MAKIAGE and SpoiledChild brands. The business collects skin profile data from consumers via a proprietary online quiz, uses AI to match products, and sells on a subscription-replenishment model. Revenue is almost entirely digital/DTC with no wholesale dependence, generating 73% gross margins.

Industry: AI-Powered Beauty / DTC Consumer Brands  ·  vs: Estée Lauder (digital), L'Oréal (digital), Function of Beauty

"Wall Street classifies ODD as a cosmetics retailer and ignores the AI data flywheel that compounds customer LTV — paying 7.7x earnings for a business growing 25% with 73% gross margins is a multi-sigma anomaly."

Four specialist lenses — spanning 10-bagger, 100-bagger, institutional scanner, and blindspot frameworks — all identify the same category error: ODD is not a beauty company, it is a data company that happens to sell beauty products. The AI skin-match engine collects proprietary data with each transaction that improves future recommendations, reduces return rates, and increases repeat purchase rates — none of this is reflected in a 7.7x PE. At $900M market cap, 25% revenue growth, 73% gross margins, and no material analyst coverage, this is precisely the kind of quality-growth orphan that generates asymmetric returns when institutions discover it.

Price $15.34 PEG 0.8 P/E 7.7 ROIC — Catalyst SpoiledChild brand revenue growth disclosure in Q2 2026 earnings (expected August 2026) — if the second brand is showing 40%+ growth independently, the sum-of-brands valuation implies significantly more than the current market cap for each brand alone; any institutional initiations of coverage would create immediate re-rating given current near-zero coverage. Watch Customer acquisition cost inflation — ODD's DTC model depends on paid digital advertising (Meta/Google) for new customer acquisition; if CAC rises faster than LTV improvement from AI matching, unit economics deteriorate and the growth-at-quality narrative breaks.

🏢 About the Business

Docebo provides an AI-powered Learning Management System (LMS) delivered as SaaS to mid-market and enterprise customers globally. Revenue is from multi-year subscription contracts for corporate training, compliance learning, and partner education programs. The company operates in the corporate LMS market with 80% gross margins and high net revenue retention.

Industry: Enterprise SaaS / Corporate Learning Management  ·  vs: Cornerstone OnDemand, SAP SuccessFactors Learning, Absorb LMS

"A profitable SaaS LMS at PEG 0.2 with 80% gross margins and zero sell-side coverage is the definition of a quality orphan — decelerating from 20% to 14% growth is not a business problem, it is an institutional attention problem."

Three specialist lenses — 10-bagger, 100-bagger, and Wall St blindspot — identify Docebo as one of the most extreme quality-at-discount combinations in the dataset: PEG of 0.2 is essentially impossible for a 14% growing SaaS business with 80% gross margins unless the market has simply not noticed it exists. At $500M market cap with 100% revenue consistency, -5% annual buyback (shares retiring), and zero net debt, this is a business compounding intrinsic value while the stock languishes due to institutional size constraints. The AI feature monetisation cycle has not begun to show in financials — it represents free optionality.

Price $16.72 PEG 0.2 P/E 12.3 ROIC — Catalyst AI feature monetisation disclosure — if Q2 2026 earnings show incremental revenue from AI Mentor/AI-powered authoring tools, the growth deceleration narrative (20% → 14%) reverses and the PEG re-rates; institutional discovery is also a catalyst given zero dedicated sell-side coverage. Watch Revenue growth decelerating further toward single digits would confirm the thesis that AI feature uplift is not materialising; any growth below 10% with 80% gross margins would suggest the LMS market is saturating for Docebo's price point, removing the 10-bagger pathway.

#11 ADBE Large Cap Adobe Inc. · Technology HIGH WITHIN MONTHS Quality Compounder Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Adobe provides creative software (Photoshop, Premiere Pro, Illustrator), document management (Acrobat/Sign), and digital marketing analytics (Experience Cloud) delivered as SaaS subscriptions. Revenue is highly recurring with 89% gross margins. Customers are creative professionals, enterprises, and marketing teams who embed Adobe workflows deeply into daily production processes.

Industry: Creative/Marketing SaaS / Generative AI Platforms  ·  vs: Canva (design), Figma (collaborative design), Salesforce Marketing Cloud

"The market priced Adobe as an AI disruption victim — but Firefly integration is deepening switching costs, and 37% ROIC at PE 13.9 is the cheapest large-cap quality compounder in software."

Two specialist lenses (Quality Growth and Scale Economics Shared) agree that the Figma deal collapse and Canva/AI disruption fears created a permanent-seeming derating that is fundamentally unjustified. Adobe's Firefly generative AI is not a threat — it is an extension of the existing platform that adds a use case without replacing the core workflow. At ROIC 37%, 89% gross margins, 42% FCF margins, and PE 13.9, this is the most asymmetric large-cap quality compounder in software — and the FCF yield of 10% comfortably clears the 4.32% rate hurdle. The PEG of 1.5 is at the entry gate boundary, which is why this is SATELLITE rather than CORE, but the quality of the business is unambiguous.

Price $244.45 PEG 1.5 P/E 13.9 ROIC 37.4% Catalyst FY2026 Q3 earnings (September 2026) Firefly monetisation metrics — if Adobe discloses Firefly API revenue or Firefly-embedded workflow engagement showing 30%+ user adoption, the AI disruption narrative inverts to AI enhancement narrative and PE re-rates toward 20x. Watch Canva raising a major funding round and entering enterprise with aggressive pricing would be the most credible competitive threat to monitor — if Canva launches a genuine enterprise suite with SSO/compliance, Adobe's SMB creative segment comes under structural pressure.

#12 HALO Mid Cap Halozyme Therapeutics, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN MONTHS Fast Grower Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Halozyme owns the ENHANZE drug delivery technology — a proprietary enzyme (rHuPH20) that enables subcutaneous delivery of biologic drugs that would otherwise require IV infusion. The company licenses this technology to pharmaceutical partners (Roche, Johnson & Johnson, AbbVie, Pfizer, Janssen) in exchange for milestone payments and royalties on drug sales. Halozyme does not have its own commercial drug pipeline risk.

Industry: Drug Delivery Technology / Pharma Royalties  ·  vs: Alteogen (emerging Korean competitor with similar ENHANZE-like technology), Halozyme has no direct royalty platform peer

"The market applies a volatile-biotech multiple to what is effectively a high-margin toll road on blockbuster drug conversions — fwd PE 7.8x versus trailing 25.3x signals the earnings inflection is massively underappreciated."

Halozyme is misclassified. It is not a drug developer — it has no clinical pipeline risk. It is a royalty company that collects fees every time a partner drug is sold in subcutaneous form. The dramatic gap between trailing PE (25.3x) and forward PE (7.8x) reflects that royalty payments from already-approved drugs (Darzalex subQ, Phesgo, Hyqvia) are ramping sharply as patient conversion from IV to subQ accelerates. At ROIC 24%, 82% gross margins, 46% FCF margins, and PEG 0.7, this business has the financial profile of a software royalty company. The biotech classification is the sole reason for the multiple discount.

Price $69.29 PEG 0.7 P/E 25.3 ROIC 23.6% Catalyst New ENHANZE partnership announcements expected H1/H2 2026 — Halozyme has historically announced 1-2 new licensing deals per year; any additional blockbuster drug conversion (the market speculates on Humira biosimilar subQ conversion) would add multiple hundreds of millions in NPV royalties at near-100% margin. Watch Alteogen technology gaining FDA approval for a competing hyaluronidase in a head-to-head scenario — if a partner company chooses Alteogen over ENHANZE for a new application, it signals competitive displacement beginning; track Alteogen regulatory submissions in the US.
Agents: 📈 Cap Appreciation

⚠ Key Risks

  • Rate re-acceleration risk: if CPI prints above 3.5% in May 2026, the 10Y could spike to 4.75%+, compressing multiples on any name without near-term FCF coverage — most damaging to ALNY (fwd PE 16x but minimal current FCF) and MNDY (ROIC near zero).
  • EM currency / geopolitical shock: DLO's thesis depends on EM payment volumes and USD/local FX stability — a sharp USD strengthening event or Brazil/Argentina policy shock would compress take-rates and trigger multiple de-rating regardless of business quality.
  • DOGE federal spending cuts materializing deeper than expected: BAH's 85% margin of safety assumes classified/defense spending is protected — if bipartisan budget reconciliation cuts IT services beyond civilian DOGE scope, the ROIC thesis degrades; watch Q2 contract awards.

🏢 About
Duolingo operates a freemium language-learning platform with over 500 million registered users, monetizing through a subscription tier (Duolingo Plus/Max), advertising, and in-app purchases. Customers are individual learners globally, with growing enterprise and English-proficiency testing (Duolingo English Test) revenue streams.

🏢 About
H&R Block provides consumer tax preparation services through ~9,000 company-owned and franchised retail offices across North America, supplemented by its DIY digital platform (Block Online). It earns revenue from per-return fees, software subscriptions, and financial products (Emerald Card, refund advance loans). Customers are primarily middle-income households filing personal tax returns seasonally.

🏢 About
PTC Therapeutics is a rare disease biopharmaceutical company developing and commercializing small molecule and gene therapies. Its commercial products include Translarna (ataluren) for Duchenne muscular dystrophy in ex-US markets and Emflaza (deflazacort) for DMD in the US, with Upstaza (eladocagene exuparvovec) for AADC deficiency. Revenue comes primarily from product sales to hospitals and specialty pharmacies serving rare disease patients.
Industry: Rare Disease / Specialty Pharma · vs: Sarepta Therapeutics, Solid Biosciences, Roche (SMA franchise)
Market is pricing PTCT at trough rare-disease multiples despite a 115% revenue inflection driven by newly-launched products, with SMA pipeline approval optionality offered for free at PE=8.7.

Industry: Specialty Insurance · vs: Kingsway Financial, Hippo Holdings, RLI Corp

HALO ▼
HIGH
Halozyme Therapeutics
Fast Grower Turnaround
52wPos: 84%. RG: +22% → +38% (re-accelerating). Catalyst: ENHANZE partner launches including argenx's VYVGART Hytrulo subcutaneous filing expansions and J&J Darzalex FASPRO label updates expected Q2 2026; forward PE comp
🏢 About
Halozyme develops and licenses its ENHANZE drug delivery technology, which uses the PEGPH20 enzyme to convert IV biologics into subcutaneous formulations for pharmaceutical partners. Revenue comes almost entirely from royalties and milestone payments from partners including Roche, Janssen, AbbVie, and Argenx. The asset-light model means royalties flow through at very high margins with no manufacturing risk.
Industry: Drug Delivery Technology / Biotech Royalties · vs: Antares Pharma, Enable Injections, Credence Medical
Halozyme is a royalty compounder masquerading as a biotech — the market applies a volatile-biotech discount to what is effectively a high-margin toll road on blockbuster drug conversions.

Customers are patients with rare genetic disorders and the cardiovascular specialists treating them.

🏢 About
Turning Point Brands sells alternative tobacco and nicotine products — including Stoker's moist snuff, Zig-Zag rolling papers, and modern oral nicotine pouches — through convenience stores and online channels across the US. It earns money on high-margin branded consumables that smokers and tobacco users repurchase repeatedly. Customers are value-oriented adult tobacco consumers who buy these products weekly.
Industry: Alternative Tobacco / Nicotine Products · vs: Altria, Swedish Match (Philip Morris), Reynolds American
Institutional ESG exclusion of tobacco keeps this 28%-revenue-growth, PEG=0.6 brand compounder permanently undervalued in plain sight.
PEG=0.6 with 28% revenue growth and 57% gross margins — consumer brand economics · $81 · $1.6B

💯 100-Bagger Framework
Christopher Mayer's criteria: small market cap (<$2B), ROIC >15%, large reinvestable TAM, owner-operator management, no dilution, and 'twin engines' of growth + multiple expansion.

🏢 About

🌍 Macro Dashboard — FRED data as of 2026-04-16

AI MARKET SYNOPSIS

The market is simultaneously mis-pricing two distinct opportunities: (1) high-quality compounders with 25-46% FCF margins are trading at deep discounts because AI disruption fears have been applied indiscriminately to every incumbent software and services business, and (2) a cluster of profitable, cash-generative small/mid-caps remain structurally invisible to institutions due to size, sector stigma, or geographic bias. With the 10Y at 4.32% and VIX at 17.9, the market is calm but not complacent — creating ideal conditions to accumulate quality names before catalysts compress discounts. The real opportunity is owning businesses with 28-37% ROIC that generate FCF yields well above the risk-free rate, at PEGs of 0.5-1.2.

🌍 GEOPOLITICAL & MACRO CONTEXT

The 10Y Treasury at 4.32% sets a genuine hurdle — every equity pick must demonstrate FCF yield above 4% or a credible growth premium that justifies the spread over risk-free. The yield curve at +55bps (normal, non-inverted) removes near-term recession signal, while unemployment at 4.3% and CPI at 3.29% suggest the Fed remains in a data-dependent hold posture rather than pivot mode. VIX at 17.9 indicates orderly markets — not a fear-driven buying opportunity, but not euphoria either — meaning valuation discipline matters more than momentum right now.
